title: verbena neomexicana var. neomexicana
intro: Verbena neomexicana var. neomexicana, also known as New Mexico verbena, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Verbena neomexicana var. neomexicana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate stress and anxiety, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
